full_response:
Let ω_{ab} be an arbitrary rank-2 tensor and U^a any 4-vector (e.g. the 4-velocity of some observer).

1. Split ω_{ab} into its symmetric and antisymmetric parts  
 ω_{ab}=ω_{(ab)}+ω_{[ab]}, where  
 ω_{(ab)}≡½(ω_{ab}+ω_{ba}), ω_{[ab]}≡½(ω_{ab}-ω_{ba}).

2. Contract with U^a U^b:  
 ω_{ab}U^aU^b=(ω_{(ab)}+ω_{[ab]})U^aU^b  
       =ω_{(ab)}U^aU^b+ω_{[ab]}U^aU^b.

3. Show the antisymmetric term vanishes.  
Because U^aU^b is symmetric in its indices,

 ω_{[ab]}U^aU^b = –ω_{[ab]}U^bU^a      (rename dummy indices a↔b)  
          = –ω_{[ab]}U^aU^b.

Hence ω_{[ab]}U^aU^b equals its own negative and must be zero:

 ω_{[ab]}U^aU^b = 0.

4. Therefore only the symmetric part contributes:

 ω_{ab}U^aU^b = ω_{(ab)}U^aU^b.

\boxed{\,\omega_{ab}U^{a}U^{b}=\omega_{(ab)}U^{a}U^{b}\,}

Confidence: 80%
